preamble: Debuccalization of voiced stops to a glottal stop is rarer than for voiceless stops, but occurs through lenition or as a specialized sound change in specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Word-final bilabial implosives in multisyllabic words shift to glottal stops.
In some Bavarian varieties, stops merged and debuccalized before nasals.

Glottal reinforcement of /b/ is rare in English dialects (voiceless stops /t/, /p/, /k/ are primary targets). When /b/ undergoes glottalization, it typically occurs pre-glottalized (bʔ) rather than replaced.
